title: heterotis rupicola
intro: Heterotis rupicola, also known as the "River Bushwillow" or "Makalani", is a deciduous tree native to the tropical regions of Africa. Its bark and leaves have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Heterotis rupicola include treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as providing protection against malaria and other diseases. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and ointments, which are often used to treat a variety of ailments in traditional African medicine.
